items.fix: Cache only proven hot paths; size and TTL each cache; monitor hit rate.
items.why: Unbounded caches grow into OOMs; low-hit-rate caches add complexity without payoff.
items.example:
```python
from functools import lru_cache
@lru_cache(maxsize=10_000)   # bounded, NOT maxsize=None
def hot_path(key): ...
```

items.mistake: Choosing a tradeoff without measuring the bottleneck.
items.fix: Profile (cProfile, py-spy, py-instrument); make the tradeoff that buys you the *measured* scarce resource.
items.why: Optimizing the wrong axis spends complexity without changing the symptom.
items.example:
```python
import cProfile
cProfile.run('endpoint()')   # find where seconds (or MB) actually go
```

items.mistake: Trading memory for time on small n.
items.fix: On tiny inputs, a tight linear scan beats hash-table construction overhead.
items.why: Big-O is asymptotic; constants and cache locality dominate at small n.
items.example:
```python
# Tiny list: a linear `if x in xs` may beat building a set first.
if x in [1, 2, 3, 4]: ...
```

items.mistake: Holding the entire dataset in memory when streaming would do.
items.fix: Use generators, iterators, and chunked reads.
items.why: Memory hogging causes GC pressure, swapping, or OOM kills on production-sized inputs.
items.example:
```python
for line in open('big.log'):    # streamed, O(1) memory
    process(line)
```

items.mistake: Treating memory as free because the machine has lots of it.
items.fix: Account for shared usage (other processes, GC, page cache, KV-cache for other requests).
items.why: Memory is fungible and contested; tomorrow's traffic doesn't care that today's box was idle.
items.example:
```python
# Bound the cache, even on big boxes:
cache = LruDict(max_bytes=2 * GB)
```
